mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly positive when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening supply-side capacity utilization, which forces firms to raise prices and boosts short-term profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (p > 0.10) in recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a tightening monetary policy stance forces central banks to maintain restrictive conditions, which suppresses risk appetite and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite the inflation-tightening combination, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.03) over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a resilient labor market that supports household income and consumption, providing a stable earnings floor that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently precedes negative 60-day equity returns, or the series exhibits zero predictive information (Rank IC ~ 0) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals a collapsing real estate investment cycle that drags down construction spending and financial sector credit quality, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing market downturns fail to transmit to equity markets, or 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial output during a monetary easing phase reflects a deliberate demand cooling strategy rather than structural weakness, allowing equity valuations to stabilize and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during easing periods consistently coincide with negative equity returns, or the regime filter fails to improve out-of-sample accuracy (Sharpe ratio < 0.5).
